content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

April 20, 1989

Lynne Andersen
Legislative Assistant
Assemblymember Filante's Office
State Capitol, Room 5135
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Letter No. 89-234

Dear Ms. Andersen:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act
was received on April 18, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices
Commission. If you have any questions about your advice request,
you may contact John McLean an attorney in the Legal Division,
directly at (916) 322-5901.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21
working days if your request seeks formal written advice. If more
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to
information needed. If your request is for informal assistance,
we will answer it as quickly as we can. (See Commission
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).)

You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,

Kathryn E. Donovan
Acting General Counsel

KED:plh

INTEROFFICE MEMORANDUM

Date: 17-Apr-1989 04:14pm PDT
From: LYNNE ANDERSEN
ANDERSEN_LY AT A1 AT AC09

Dept:
Tel No:

TO: Debbie Spagnoli ( SPAGNOLI_DE AT A1 AT AMIN1 )

Subject: regcard

MEMORANDUM

TO: John McLean
Fair Political Practices Commission

FROM: Lynne Andersen, Legislative Assistant

DATE: April 17, 1989

RE: 9th Assembly District Small Business Conference

Enclosed is a pamphlet, to be produced at state expense, for
our 1989 Small Business Conference to be held in San Rafael
on May 13, 1989. The pamphlet has been approved under the
public meeting exemption by Pat Foster in the Legislative
Counsel's office.

I would like approval by the FPPC, in writing, under the
public meeting exemption. The pamphlet would be sent to
approximately 12,000 small business owners in the 9th A.D. I
would also like to clarify a few parts of the pamphlet:

1) The pamphlet would be three-fold, mailed bulk rate
in the member's envelopes. The front page indicates
that the conference is sponsored by the member. If
approved, can an Assembly and/or State seal be used?

2) The agenda page lists three small business topics the

member will address, in addition to question and
answers on additional legislative matters.

3) The "Open Letter" expands on some of those small
business issues.

4) The registration card lists the Marin County Chamber
of Commerce as the return card recipient, as
recommended by the Legislative Counsel's Office.

5) The cost of the conference covers room rental and
refreshments only.

Thank you for your review. I would like an answer as soon as
possible, as the conference date is rapidly approaching.
Please contact me with any questions you may have at 5-7827.

1989 SMALL BUSINESS CONFERENCE


Sponsored by

ASSEMBLYMAN BILL FILANTE, M.D.
9TH ASSEMBLY DISTRICT


In cooperation with

Marin and Sonoma County Chambers of Commerce


Saturday, May 13, 1989
8:15 a.m. to 12:30 p.m.

Dominican College
Caleruega Hall
1520 Grand Avenue
San Rafael

AGENDA

8:00 a.m.  COFFEE AND REGISTRATION
8:30 a.m.  LEGISLATIVE UPDATE:
            ASSEMBLYMAN BILL FILANTE, M.D.

            Discuss these topics and more
            with Bill:
            1) Mandated Health Benefits
            2) Workers Compensation
            3) Parental Leave

8:45 a.m.  "Productivity and Profitability
            in the Marketplace"
            -Mimi Long, Telemarketing Manager
            Pacific Bell
9:15 a.m.  "Solutions to the Workers Comp
            Debate"
            -Roy Truelsen, Vice President of
            Legislation and Legal Counsel,
            Consolidated Freightways
9:45 a.m.  Questions and Answers

10:00 a.m. Break/Information Faire

10:30 a.m. "Personal Computers and Office
            Automation"
            -Joe Brilando, Vice-President
            of Marketing and Strategy,
            Ashton-Tate Corp., Anaheim

11:00 a.m. "America, Inc.: Formula for
            Success in the Pacific Century"
            -Michael Serwetz, President
            Pacific Century Associates &
            World-Wide Apparel Services

11:30 a.m. Panel discussion

12:30 a.m. Closing

AN OPEN LETTER TO ALL 9TH A.D
SMALL BUSINESSES:


     It is my pleasure to invite you to
a conference designed for the small
business owner.

     I am bringing together speakers
and panelists on a variety of topics
to help in development of your
business, help in computer
technology, and a discussion on the
workers compensation and mandated
health insurance debates currently
raging in Sacramento.

     Here in the North Bay, small
business accounts for much of our
economic prosperity.  However, these
same small firms feel the worst
pinch from excessive government
regulation.

     Mandated health insurance for
employers of five people or more is
a real possibility this year.  As
chairman of a legislative task
force, I am working on a solution
w th peop e m st affected, namely
emp ye s nsurers, hea th care
gr ups and c nsumers.

     In order to find the best plan to
provide hea th care w thout
bankrupting sma  business, I need
your input.  Please fill out and
return the attached RSVP card.  I
look forward to seeing you on May
13th.

          Sincerely,


          BILL FILANTE, M.D.

INFORMATION FAIRE

Come and Meet Representatives From:

California State University

State Department of General Services

State Department of Transportation

State Board of Equalization

State Employment Development
Department

State Department of Corrections

State Franchise Tax Board

U.S. Small Business Administration

Aston-Tate Corporation, Anaheim

Pacific Bell

World-Wide Apparel Services

Pacific Century Associates

For further information call: (415)479-4920, (707)762-5706,

to 5th Avenue, left on 5th to
Grand Avenue, left on Grand to
Locust, right on Locust,
straight to Caleruega Hall.

From the South: From Hwy. 101,
take Irwin Street exit to 5th
Avenue, right on 5th to Grand,
left on Grand to Locust, right
on Locust, straight to
Caleruega Hall.

I would like approval by the FPPC, in writing, under the

1) The pamphlet would be three-fold, mailed bulk rate

AN OPEN LETTER TO ALL 9TH A.D
SMALL BUSINESSES:

It is my pleasure to invite you to
a conference designed for the small
business owner.

I am bringing together speakers
and panelists on a variety of topics
to help in development of your
business, help in computer
technology, and a discussion on the
workers compensation and mandated
health insurance debates currently
raging in Sacramento.

Here in the North Bay, small
business accounts for much of our
economic prosperity. However, these
same small firms feel the worst
pinch from excessive government
regulation.

Mandated health insurance for
employers of five people or more is
a real possibility this year. As
chairman of a legislative task
force, I am working on a solution
w th people most affected, namely
emp oyees insurers, health care
groups and consumers.

In order to find the best plan to
provide health care without
bankrupting small business, I need
your input. Please fill out and
return the attached RSVP card. I
look forward to seeing you on May
13th.

Sincerely,

BILL FILANTE, M.D.

STATE COUNCIL ON DEVELOPMENTAL DISABILITIES

June 15, 1988


California Fair Political Practices
Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Attention: Legal Division

To whom it may concern:

As recommended by Linda Moureaux of your Technical Assistance and
Analysis Division, per our telephone conversation of June 14, 1988, the
State Council on Developmental Disabilities (Council) requests an
"advice letter" on the following situation:

     Council members include the Secretary of the Health and Welfare
Agency, Director of Developmental Services, Director of Rehabilitation and Superintendent of Public Instruction (Section 4520-4523 of
the Welfare & Institutions Code). At present, representatives
and/or alternates have been designated:

MEMBER                                REPRESENTATIVE              ALTERNATE

Secretary of the                   Barbara Hooker
  Health & Welfare Agency          Assistant Secretary
  Clifford L. Allenby

Director of                                                    Al Lee
  Developmental Services                                       Chief Deputy
  Gary D. Macomber                                            Director

Director of                        Merrill Jacobs
  Rehabilitation                   Chief Deputy Director
  P. Cecilio Fontanoza

Superintendent of Public           Kathryn Summers
  Instruction                       Special Education
  Bill Honig


DD  1507-21st STREET • ROOM 320 • SACRAMENTO, CA 95814 • TELEPHONE (916) 322-8481

DOC 2 (8/82)

FPPC
June 15, 1988
Page 2


Each of the designated representatives/alternates have voting
privileges at Council meetings.

Due to numerous commitments, the Secretary of the Health and
Welfare Agency, Director of Rehabilitation and Superintendent of
Public Instruction have not attended past Council meetings. It is
not known, at this time, when and if these individuals will person-
ally attend future meetings.

Basically, the question is who should file? See enclosed letter
dated June 15, 1988, addressed to Ms. Brown, Technical Assistance
and Analysis Division.

Your attention to this matter is appreciated. If further information is
needed, please do not hesitate to contact me at (916) 322-8481.

Sincerely,

[signature]
ARLENE A. NISHIMURA
Executive Secretary

AAN:aan

Enclosure

cc: Clifford L. Allenby
    James F. Bellotti

STATE COUNCIL ON DEVELOPMENTAL DISABILITIES
June 15, 1988


Betty M. Brown
Legal Documents Examiner
Technical Assistance and Analysis Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807
                                                Name: CLIFFORD L. ALLENBY
                                                Re: Statement of Economic Interests
                                                Type of Statement: Assuming Office
                                                Date of Statement Due: March 3, 1988

Dear Ms. Brown:

As per my telephone conversation with Linda Moureaux of your staff on
June 14, 1988, the following is an explanation as to why Mr. Clifford L.
Allenby's Form 730 Assuming Office statement was not filed in a timely
manner:

     The Secretary of the Health and Welfare Agency (HWA) is, by statute
     (Section 4520-4523 of the Welfare & Institutions Code), a member of
     the State Council on Developmental Disabilities (SCDD). Mr.
     Allenby was appointed as Agency Secretary in 1986. Ms. Barbara
     Hooker, Assistant HWA Secretary, represents the HWA Secretary with
     voting privileges at all Council meetings. Prior to Mr. Allenby's
     assuming office, Ms. Hooker was the HWA Secretary's representative.

On January 12, 1987, I requested Mr. Allenby file a Form 730 as a
member of SCDD. Shortly thereafter, I recall receiving a phone
call from a member of Mr. Allenby's staff stating that Mr. Allenby
did not need to file as Ms. Barbara Hooker was his designated
representative. I then called FPPC requesting clarification and
spoke to you on January 14, 1987. My notes indicate that you
explained: 1) Only representatives file; and 2) Alternates and
Directors must both file. Based on this information, I did not
pursue Mr. Allenby's Form 730 as Ms. Hooker was his designated
representative.

On January 29, 1988, I spoke with Lynn Martin of your staff, and it
was explained to me that all members must file a Form 730,

DD    1507-21st STREET - ROOM 320 • SACRAMENTO, CA 95814 • TELEPHONE (916) 322-8481

DOC 2(8/82)

AN OPEN LETTER TO ALL 9TH A.D 
SMALL BUSINESSES: 
It is my pleasure to invite you to 
a conference designed for the small 
business owner. 
I am bringing together speakers 
and panelists on a variety of topics 
to help in development of your 
business, help in computer 
technology, and a discussion on the 
workers compensation and mandated 
health insurance debates currently 
raging in Sacramento. 
Here in the North Bay, small 
business accounts for much of our 
economic prosperity. 
However, these 
same small firms feel the worst 
pinch from excessive government 
regulation. 
Mandated health insurance for 
employers of five people or more is 
a real possibility this year. 
As 
chairman of a legislative task 
force, I 
am working on a solution

Dear Ms. Nishimura: 
Re: 
88-234 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform 
Act was received on June 21, 1988 by the Fair Political 
Practices commission.
embedding.first_500_words: California Fair Political Practices Commission April 20, 1989 Lynne Andersen Legislative Assistant Assemblymember Filante's Office State Capitol, Room 5135 Sacramento, CA 95814 Re: Letter No. 89-234 Dear Ms. Andersen: Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act was received on April 18, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices Commission. If you have any questions about your advice request, you may contact John McLean an attorney in the Legal Division, directly at (916) 322-5901. We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore, unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written advice. If more information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to information needed. If your request is for informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we can. (See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).) You also should be aware that your letter and our response are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon receipt of a proper request for disclosure. Very truly yours, Kathryn E. Donovan Acting General Counsel KED:plh INTEROFFICE MEMORANDUM Date: 17-Apr-1989 04:14pm PDT From: LYNNE ANDERSEN ANDERSEN_LY AT A1 AT AC09 Dept: Tel No: TO: Debbie Spagnoli ( SPAGNOLI_DE AT A1 AT AMIN1 ) Subject: regcard MEMORANDUM TO: John McLean Fair Political Practices Commission FROM: Lynne Andersen, Legislative Assistant DATE: April 17, 1989 RE: 9th Assembly District Small Business Conference Enclosed is a pamphlet, to be produced at state expense, for our 1989 Small Business Conference to be held in San Rafael on May 13, 1989. The pamphlet has been approved under the public meeting exemption by Pat Foster in the Legislative Counsel's office. I would like approval by the FPPC, in writing, under the public meeting exemption. The pamphlet would be sent to approximately 12,000 small business owners in the 9th A.D. I would also like to clarify a few parts of the pamphlet: 1) The pamphlet would be three-fold, mailed bulk rate in the member's envelopes. The front page indicates that the conference is sponsored by the member. If approved, can an Assembly and/or State seal be used? 2) The agenda page lists three small business topics the member will address, in addition to question and answers on additional legislative matters. 3) The "Open Letter" expands on some of those small business issues. 4) The registration card lists the Marin County Chamber of Commerce as the return card recipient, as recommended by the Legislative Counsel's Office. 5) The cost of the conference covers room rental and refreshments only. Thank you for your review. I would like an answer as soon as possible, as the conference date is rapidly approaching. Please contact me with any questions you may have at 5-7827. 1989 SMALL BUSINESS CONFERENCE Sponsored by ASSEMBLYMAN BILL FILANTE, M.D. 9TH ASSEMBLY DISTRICT In cooperation with Marin and Sonoma County Chambers
